name: Post-night sleep protection
when_it_applies: After a night shift ends, especially before a rapid flip.
when_not_to_use: If there is an unavoidable critical obligation within 2 hours.
evidence_note: NIOSH notes protected recovery sleep reduces fatigue accumulation.

name: Strategic nap window
when_it_applies: When sleep opportunity is short or before a high-risk commute.
when_not_to_use: Within 3 hours of planned main sleep if not urgently needed.
evidence_note: Short naps improve alertness with limited sleep inertia.

name: Caffeine cutoff
when_it_applies: During shift to preserve later sleep quality.
evidence_note: Caffeine close to sleep reduces sleep depth and duration.

name: Bright light after wake
when_it_applies: Within 30 minutes of waking for alertness and phase support.
when_not_to_use: Immediately after shift if planning to sleep soon.
evidence_note: Timed bright light is a strong circadian cue.

name: Dim light before sleep
when_it_applies: Final hour before planned sleep after evening/night activity.
when_not_to_use: If immediate safety tasks require full alertness.
evidence_note: Light reduction supports melatonin signaling and sleep onset.

name: Decompression walk
when_it_applies: After stressful shift before entering sleep routine.
when_not_to_use: If very sleep deprived and unsafe outdoors alone.
evidence_note: Brief decompression improves transition from work stress to recovery.

name: Three-minute wind-down
when_it_applies: When there is little time but stress is high.
when_not_to_use: Not a replacement for core sleep blocks.
evidence_note: Short breathing/relaxation lowers arousal and improves sleep readiness.

name: Protect-this-block message
when_it_applies: Before anchor sleep to reduce interruptions from family/team.
when_not_to_use: If social support system is unavailable.
evidence_note: Pre-commitment and communication reduce sleep disruption.

name: Buddy check-in
when_it_applies: High strain days, post-night commute, or missed anchors.
when_not_to_use: If user opts out of buddy sharing.
evidence_note: Social support buffers stress and improves adherence.

name: Unsafe drive-home alert
when_it_applies: After night shift with long commute or severe sleep debt.
when_not_to_use: When not driving and safe transport is already arranged.
evidence_note: Post-night driving carries elevated drowsy driving risk.

name: Anchor block recovery
when_it_applies: When plan mode is Protect and anchors are at risk.
when_not_to_use: If anchors already completed and risk is low.
evidence_note: Anchor sleep and recovery habits stabilize high-strain windows.

name: Meal timing reset
when_it_applies: Transitioning between nights and days with GI disruption.
evidence_note: Meal timing consistency supports circadian adaptation.
